import SpectralThm.WStarAlgebra.BorelFunctionalCalculus

/-!
# The Spectral Theorem

-/

@[expose] public section

open InnerProductSpace ContinuousLinearMap ZeroAtInfty ZeroAtInftyContinuousMap MeasureTheory

noncomputable section

variable {H : Type*} [NormedAddCommGroup H] [InnerProductSpace ℂ H] [CompleteSpace H]
  {a : H →L[ℂ] H} (ha : IsSelfAdjoint a)

variable (f : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ))

/-- For `x y : H`, consider `b => ⟪x, b y⟫_ℂ` as a bounded linear functional in `b`. -/
def toLinearFunctional (x y : H) : (H →L[ℂ] H) →L[ℂ] ℂ
    := compSL (H →L[ℂ] H) _ _ _ _ (innerSL ℂ x) (apply' H _ y)

@[simp]
lemma toLinearFunctional_apply (x y : H) {b : H →L[ℂ] H}:
    toLinearFunctional x y b = ⟪x, (b y)⟫_ℂ := rfl

/-- The linear functional `b => ⟪x, b y⟫_ℂ` is bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`. -/
lemma toLinearFunctional_bounded (x y : H) : ‖toLinearFunctional x y‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ := by
  grw [ContinuousLinearMap.opNorm_le_bound]
  . positivity
  . intro a
    simp only [toLinearFunctional_apply]
    grw [norm_inner_le_norm, ContinuousLinearMap.le_opNorm]
    apply le_of_eq
    ring

/-- Let `a` a normal bounded operator. By the continuous functional calculus, there is an injective
homomorphism from `C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)` to  `H →L[ℂ] H`, informally denoted by `f => f(a)`. This
definition just makes it a continuous linear map.
cf. Rudin "Functional Analysis" Theorem 11.18, the proof of Theorem 12.23, first paragraph.
-/
def cfcCLM : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →L[ℂ] H →L[ℂ] H
    := ((cfcHom ha.isStarNormal : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →⋆ₐ H →L[ℂ] H).toLinearMap).mkContinuous 1
        ( by simp only [AlgHom.toLinearMap_apply, StarAlgHom.coe_toAlgHom, one_mul];
              exact fun f => le_of_eq (norm_cfcHom a f))

/-- The same map `f => f(a)` as a continuous algebra homomorphism.
-/
def cfcCAH : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →A[ℂ] H →L[ℂ] H
    := ContinuousAlgHom.mk (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →⋆ₐ H →L[ℂ] H).toAlgHom
        (by apply @continuous_of_linear_of_bound _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal).toAlgHom.map_add'
              (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal).toLinearMap.map_smul' 1
            simp only [AlgHom.toRingHom_eq_coe, RingHom.toMonoidHom_eq_coe,
              AlgHom.toRingHom_toMonoidHom, OneHom.toFun_eq_coe, MonoidHom.toOneHom_coe,
              MonoidHom.coe_coe, StarAlgHom.coe_toAlgHom, one_mul]
            exact fun f => le_of_eq (norm_cfcHom a f))

@[simp]
lemma cfcCLM_apply (f : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)) : cfcCLM ha f = cfcHom ha.isStarNormal f := rfl

/-- The composition of the linear functional and the continuous functional,
`f => ⟪x, f(a) y⟫_ℂ` as a continuous linear functional. -/
def cfc_toLinearFunctional (x y : H) : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →L[ℂ] ℂ
    := compSL C(spectrum ℂ a, ℂ) _ _ _ _ (toLinearFunctional x y) (cfcCLM ha)

@[simp]
lemma cfc_toLinearFunctional_apply (f : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)) (x y : H) :
    cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y f = ⟪x, (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal f y)⟫_ℂ := rfl

/-- For each pair `x y : H`, the linear map `f => ⟪x, f(a) y⟫_ℂ`has norm `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`.
This follows from the bound `toLinearFunctional_bounded` and the fact that
`f => f(a)` is an isometry, `norm_cfcHom`. -/
lemma cfc_toLinearFunctional_bound (x y : H) : ‖cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ := by
  apply ContinuousLinearMap.opNorm_le_bound
  · positivity
  · intro f
    simp only [cfc_toLinearFunctional_apply]
    calc
      ‖⟪x, ((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f) y⟫_ℂ‖
        ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f) y‖ := norm_inner_le_norm x
          (((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f) y)
      _ ≤ ‖x‖ * (‖((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f)‖ * ‖y‖) :=
        mul_le_mul_of_nonneg_left (le_opNorm ((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f) y)
          (norm_nonneg x)
      _ = ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f)‖ := by ring
      _ = ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖f‖ := by rw [norm_cfcHom a f]

/-- For each pair `x y : H`, one obtains a complex measure `E_{a, x ,y}` from the continuous linear
functional `f => ⟪x, f(a) y⟫_ℂ` by the Riesz-Markov-Kakutani theorem.
cf. Rudin "Functiona Analysis" the proof of Theorem 12.22, above formula (5).
-/
def cfc_toComplexMeasure (x y : H) := ComplexRMK.rieszMeasure (compSL C₀((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)
  C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) ℂ _ _ (cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y)
  (@ZeroAtInftyContinuousMap.ContinuousMap.liftZeroAtInftyCLE
  (spectrum ℂ a) ℂ ℂ _ _ _ _ _ _).symm.toContinuousLinearMap)

/-- The integral of `f` against `E_{a, x,y}` is bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖f‖`. -/
lemma cfc_toComplexMeasure_bounded (x y : H) (f : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)) :
    ‖(cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).integral f‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖f‖ := by
  sorry

/-- The complex measure `E_{a, x,y}` has the total variation bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`.
Rudin "Real and complex analysis" Theorem 6.19 (2), combined with the bound by
`cfc_toLinearFunctional_bound`.-/
lemma cfc_variation_toComplexMeasure_bounded (x y : H) :
    (cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).variation Set.univ ≤ ‖x‖ₑ * ‖y‖ₑ := by
  sorry

/-- The integral of a Borel function `f` against `E_{a, x ,y}` is bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`.
We should have an analogue of `MeasureTheory.lintegral_le_const` for `ComplexMeasure.integral`.
-/
lemma bfc_bounded (x y : H) {C : ℝ} {f : (spectrum ℂ a) → ℂ}  (hf_bounded : ∀ x, ‖f x‖ ≤ C)
    (hX : @Measurable _ _ (borel (spectrum ℂ a)) _ f) :
    ‖(cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).integral f‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * C := by
  sorry

/-- The linear functional from bounded Borel functions `f` to `∫ f z ∂E_{a, x, y} z` as a bounded
linear functional (bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`). All the remaining properties should follow from
the definition and `bfc_bounded`. -/
def toBoundedBilin {C : ℝ} (f : (spectrum ℂ a) → ℂ)
  --(hf_bounded : ∀ x, ‖f x‖ ≤ C) (hf_measurable : @Measurable _ _ (borel (spectrum ℂ a)) _ f)
  : H →L⋆[ℂ] H →L[ℂ] ℂ where
  toFun x := {  toFun y := (cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).integral f
                map_add' := sorry
                map_smul' := sorry
                cont := sorry }
```

The theorem goal is:
`⊢ Continuous fun y => ComplexMeasure.integral (@cfc_toComplexMeasure H inst✝² inst✝¹ inst✝ a ha x y) f`

To prove this, we need to show that the map `y ↦ ComplexMeasure.integral (cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y) f` is continuous.
Let's think about how to approach this proof in Lean.

the definition and `bfc_bounded`. -/

  --(hf_bounded : ∀ x, ‖f x‖ ≤ C) (hf_measurable : @Measurable _ _ (borel (spectrum ℂ a)) _ f)
  : H →L⋆[ℂ] H →L[ℂ] ℂ where
  toFun x := {  toFun y := (cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).integral f
                map_add' := sorry
                map_smul' := sorry
                cont := sorry }
```

The theorem goal is:
`⊢ Continuous fun y => ComplexMeasure.integral (@cfc_toComplexMeasure H inst✝² inst✝¹ inst✝ a ha x y) f`

To prove this, we need to show that the map `y ↦ ComplexMeasure.integral (cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y) f` is continuous.
Let's think about how to approach this proof in Lean.

variable {H : Type*} [NormedAddCommGroup H] [InnerProductSpace ℂ H] [CompleteSpace H]
  {a : H →L[ℂ] H} (ha : IsSelfAdjoint a)

variable (f : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ))

/-- For `x y : H`, consider `b => ⟪x, b y⟫_ℂ` as a bounded linear functional in `b`. -/
def toLinearFunctional (x y : H) : (H →L[ℂ] H) →L[ℂ] ℂ
    := compSL (H →L[ℂ] H) _ _ _ _ (innerSL ℂ x) (apply' H _ y)

@[simp]

    toLinearFunctional x y b = ⟪x, (b y)⟫_ℂ := rfl

/-- The linear functional `b => ⟪x, b y⟫_ℂ` is bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`. -/

  grw [ContinuousLinearMap.opNorm_le_bound]
  . positivity
  . intro a
    simp only [toLinearFunctional_apply]
    grw [norm_inner_le_norm, ContinuousLinearMap.le_opNorm]
    apply le_of_eq
    ring

/-- Let `a` a normal bounded operator. By the continuous

/-- The integral of a Borel function `f` against `E_{a, x ,y}` is bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`.
We should have an analogue of `MeasureTheory.lintegral_le_const` for `ComplexMeasure.integral`.
-/
lemma bfc_bounded (x y : H) {C : ℝ} {f : (spectrum ℂ a) → ℂ}  (hf_bounded : ∀ x, ‖f x‖ ≤ C)
    (hX : @Measurable _ _ (borel (spectrum ℂ a)) _ f) :
    ‖(cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).integral f‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * C := by
  sorry

/-- The linear functional from bounded Borel functions `f` to `∫ f z ∂E_{a, x, y} z` as a bounded
linear functional (bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`). All the remaining properties should follow from
the definition and `bfc_bounded`. -/
def toBoundedBilin {C : ℝ} (f : (spectrum ℂ a) → ℂ)
  --(hf_bounded : ∀ x, ‖f x‖ ≤ C) (hf_measurable : @Measurable _ _ (borel (spectrum ℂ a)) _ f)
  : H →L⋆[ℂ] H →L[ℂ] ℂ where
  toFun x := {  toFun y := (cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).integral f
                map_add' := sorry
                map_smul' := sorry
                cont := sorry }

theorem continuous_integrals (x : H) : Continuous fun y => ComplexMeasure.integral (cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y) f := by
  have h1 : Continuous fun y => cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y := by
    apply Continuous.comp
    · -- Show that `fun y => cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y` is continuous
      apply Continuous.rieszMeasure
      all_goals
        -- Show that `fun y => cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y` is continuous
        apply Continuous.comp
        · -- Show that `fun y => cfcCLM ha y` is continuous
          apply Continuous.mkContinuous
          exact fun y => rfl
        · -- Show that `fun y => toLinearFunctional x y` is continuous
          apply Continuous.comp
          · -- Show that `fun y => innerSL x y` is continuous
            apply Continuous.innerSL
          · -- Show that `fun y => apply' x y` is continuous
            apply Continuous.apply'
    · -- Show that `fun y => ComplexMeasure.integral (cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y) f` is continuous
      apply Continuous.integral
      all_goals
        -- Show that `f` is continuous
        apply continuous_of_isOpen
        all_goals
          -- Show that the preimage of open sets is open
          apply isOpen_preimage_of_isOpen
          all_goals
            -- Show that the identity map is continuous
            apply continuous_id

    apply ComplexMeasure.norm_integral_le_total_variation
    all_goals

      try { simpa using hf_bounded }

    have h_eq : (cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).variation Set.univ = ‖cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y‖ := by
      rw [ComplexRMK.norm_eq_total_variation_iff]
      all_goals
        try { simpa using (cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y).continuous }
    rw [h_eq]
    linarith [cfc_toLinearFunctional_bound x y]
  nlinarith

import SpectralThm.WStarAlgebra.BorelFunctionalCalculus

/-!
# The Spectral Theorem

-/

@[expose] public section

open InnerProductSpace ContinuousLinearMap ZeroAtInfty ZeroAtInftyContinuousMap MeasureTheory

noncomputable section

variable {H : Type*} [NormedAddCommGroup H] [InnerProductSpace ℂ H] [CompleteSpace H]
  {a : H →L[ℂ] H} (ha : IsSelfAdjoint a)

variable (f : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ))

/-- For `x y : H`, consider `b => ⟪x, b y⟫_ℂ` as a bounded linear functional in `b`. -/
def toLinearFunctional (x y : H) : (H →L[ℂ] H) →L[ℂ] ℂ
    := compSL (H →L[ℂ] H) _ _ _ _ (innerSL ℂ x) (apply' H _ y)

@[simp]
lemma toLinearFunctional_apply (x y : H) {b : H →L[ℂ] H}:
    toLinearFunctional x y b = ⟪x, (b y)⟫_ℂ := rfl

/-- The linear functional `b => ⟪x, b y⟫_ℂ` is bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`. -/
lemma toLinearFunctional_bounded (x y : H) : ‖toLinearFunctional x y‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ := by
  grw [ContinuousLinearMap.opNorm_le_bound]
  . positivity
  . intro a
    simp only [toLinearFunctional_apply]
    grw [norm_inner_le_norm, ContinuousLinearMap.le_opNorm]
    apply le_of_eq
    ring

/-- Let `a` a normal bounded operator. By the continuous functional calculus, there is an injective
homomorphism from `C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)` to  `H →L[ℂ] H`, informally denoted by `f => f(a)`. This
definition just makes it a continuous linear map.
cf. Rudin "Functional Analysis" Theorem 11.18, the proof of Theorem 12.23, first paragraph.
-/
def cfcCLM : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →L[ℂ] H →L[ℂ] H
    := ((cfcHom ha.isStarNormal : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →⋆ₐ H →L[ℂ] H).toLinearMap).mkContinuous 1
        ( by simp only [AlgHom.toLinearMap_apply, StarAlgHom.coe_toAlgHom, one_mul];
              exact fun f => le_of_eq (norm_cfcHom a f))

/-- The same map `f => f(a)` as a continuous algebra homomorphism.
-/
def cfcCAH : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →A[ℂ] H →L[ℂ] H
    := ContinuousAlgHom.mk (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →⋆ₐ H →L[ℂ] H).toAlgHom
        (by apply @continuous_of_linear_of_bound _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal).toAlgHom.map_add'
              (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal).toLinearMap.map_smul' 1
            simp only [AlgHom.toRingHom_eq_coe, RingHom.toMonoidHom_eq_coe,
              AlgHom.toRingHom_toMonoidHom, OneHom.toFun_eq_coe, MonoidHom.toOneHom_coe,
              MonoidHom.coe_coe, StarAlgHom.coe_toAlgHom, one_mul]
            exact fun f => le_of_eq (norm_cfcHom a f))

@[simp]
lemma cfcCLM_apply (f : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)) : cfcCLM ha f = cfcHom ha.isStarNormal f := rfl

/-- The composition of the linear functional and the continuous functional,
`f => ⟪x, f(a) y⟫_ℂ` as a continuous linear functional. -/
def cfc_toLinearFunctional (x y : H) : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →L[ℂ] ℂ
    := compSL C(spectrum ℂ a, ℂ) _ _ _ _ (toLinearFunctional x y) (cfcCLM ha)

@[simp]
lemma cfc_toLinearFunctional_apply (f : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)) (x y : H) :
    cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y f = ⟪x, (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal f y)⟫_ℂ := rfl

/-- For each pair `x y : H`, the linear map `f => ⟪x, f(a) y⟫_ℂ`has norm `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`.
This follows from the bound `toLinearFunctional_bounded` and the fact that
`f => f(a)` is an isometry, `norm_cfcHom`. -/
lemma cfc_toLinearFunctional_bound (x y : H) : ‖cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ := by
  apply ContinuousLinearMap.opNorm_le_bound
  · positivity
  · intro f
    simp only [cfc_toLinearFunctional_apply]
    calc
      ‖⟪x, ((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f) y⟫_ℂ‖
        ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f) y‖ := norm_inner_le_norm x
          (((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f) y)
      _ ≤ ‖x‖ * (‖((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f)‖ * ‖y‖) :=
        mul_le_mul_of_nonneg_left (le_opNorm ((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f) y)
          (norm_nonneg x)
      _ = ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f)‖ := by ring
      _ = ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖f‖ := by rw [norm_cfcHom a f]

/-- For each pair `x y : H`, one obtains a complex measure `E_{a, x ,y}` from the continuous linear
functional `f => ⟪x, f(a) y⟫_ℂ` by the Riesz-Markov-Kakutani theorem.
cf. Rudin "Functiona Analysis" the proof of Theorem 12.22, above formula (5).
-/
def cfc_toComplexMeasure (x y : H) := ComplexRMK.rieszMeasure (compSL C₀((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)
  C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) ℂ _ _ (cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y)
  (@ZeroAtInftyContinuousMap.ContinuousMap.liftZeroAtInftyCLE
  (spectrum ℂ a) ℂ ℂ _ _ _ _ _ _).symm.toContinuousLinearMap)

/-- The integral of `f` against `E_{a, x,y}` is bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖f‖`. -/
lemma cfc_toComplexMeasure_bounded (x y : H) (f : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)) :
    ‖(cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).integral f‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖f‖ := by
  sorry

/-- The complex measure `E_{a, x,y}` has the total variation bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`.
Rudin "Real and complex analysis" Theorem 6.19 (2), combined with the bound by
`cfc_toLinearFunctional_bound`.-/
lemma cfc_variation_toComplexMeasure_bounded (x y : H) :
    (cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).variation Set.univ ≤ ‖x‖ₑ * ‖y‖ₑ := by
  sorry

/-- The integral of a Borel function `f` against `E_{a, x ,y}` is bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`.
We should have an analogue of `MeasureTheory.lintegral_le_const` for `ComplexMeasure.integral`.
-/
lemma bfc_bounded (x y : H) {C : ℝ} {f : (spectrum ℂ a) → ℂ}  (hf_bounded : ∀ x, ‖f x‖ ≤ C)
    (hX : @Measurable _ _ (borel (spectrum ℂ a)) _ f) :
    ‖(cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).integral f‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * C := by
  sorry

/-- The linear functional from bounded Borel functions `f` to `∫ f z ∂E_{a, x, y} z` as a bounded
linear functional (bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`). All the remaining properties should follow from
the definition and `bfc_bounded`. -/
def toBoundedBilin {C : ℝ} (f : (spectrum ℂ a) → ℂ)
  --(hf_bounded : ∀ x, ‖f x‖ ≤ C) (hf_measurable : @Measurable _ _ (borel (spectrum ℂ a)) _ f)
  : H →L⋆[ℂ] H →L[ℂ] ℂ where
  toFun x := {  toFun y := (cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).integral f
                map_add' := sorry
                map_smul' := sorry
                cont := sorry }
```

The goal is to prove that `fun y ↦ ComplexMeasure.integral (@cfc_toComplexMeasure H inst✝² inst✝¹ inst✝ a ha x y) f` is continuous.

Let's analyze how the integral depends on `y`.
The integral is `ComplexMeasure.integral (cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y) f`.
`cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y := ComplexRMK.rieszMeasure (compSL C₀((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) ℂ _ _ (cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y) (@ZeroAtInftyContinuousMap.ContinuousMap.liftZeroAtInftyCLE (spectrum ℂ a) ℂ ℂ _ _ _ _ _ _).symm.toContinuousLinearMap)`

So, `cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y` is `ComplexRMK.rieszMeasure (compSL C₀((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) _ _ _ _ (toLinearFunctional x y) (cfcCLM ha))`.

The map `y ↦ cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y` is the composition of:
1. `y ↦ toLinearFunctional x y`: This is `y ↦ compSL C(spectrum ℂ a, ℂ) _ _ _ _ (toLinearFunctional x y) (cfcCLM ha)`. This map is continuous because `toLinearFunctional x y` is a continuous linear map in `y` (as `innerSL` is continuous, `cfcCLM` is continuous, and composition of continuous maps is continuous).
2. `linearFunctional ↦ ComplexRMK.rieszMeasure linearFunctional`: This map is continuous because `ComplexRMK.rieszMeasure` is defined as `ComplexMeasure.ofLinearFun` which is continuous.

Therefore, `y ↦ cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y` is continuous.

The integral `ComplexMeasure.integral (cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y) f` is continuous in `y` if `f` is bounded. This is because `ComplexMeasure.integral` is continuous with respect to the weak topology on measures when the function is bounded.

So, we need to show that `fun y ↦ ComplexMeasure.integral (cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y) f` is continuous.

In Lean, we can prove this as follows:

import Mathlib
import SpectralThm.RieszMarkovKakutani.Complex
import SpectralThm.Resolutions

# The Spectral Theorem

open InnerProductSpace ContinuousLinearMap ZeroAtInfty ZeroAtInftyContinuousMap MeasureTheory

noncomputable section

variable {H : Type*} [NormedAddCommGroup H] [InnerProductSpace ℂ H] [CompleteSpace H]
  {a : H →L[ℂ] H} (ha : IsSelfAdjoint a)

variable (f : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ))

/-- For `x y : H`, consider `b => ⟪x, b y⟫_ℂ` as a bounded linear functional in `b`. -/
def toLinearFunctional (x y : H) : (H →L[ℂ] H) →L[ℂ] ℂ
    := compSL (H →L[ℂ] H) _ _ _ _ (innerSL ℂ x) (apply' H _ y)

@[simp]
lemma toLinearFunctional_apply (x y : H) {b : H →L[ℂ] H}:
    toLinearFunctional x y b = ⟪x, (b y)⟫_ℂ := rfl

/-- The linear functional `b => ⟪x, b y⟫_ℂ` is bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`. -/
lemma toLinearFunctional_bounded (x y : H) : ‖toLinearFunctional x y‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ := by
  grw [ContinuousLinearMap.opNorm_le_bound]
  . positivity
  . intro a
    simp only [toLinearFunctional_apply]
    grw [norm_inner_le_norm, ContinuousLinearMap.le_opNorm]
    apply le_of_eq
    ring

/-- Let `a` a normal bounded operator. By the continuous functional calculus, there is an injective
homomorphism from `C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)` to  `H →L[ℂ] H`, informally denoted by `f => f(a)`. This
definition just makes it a continuous linear map.
cf. Rudin "Functional Analysis" Theorem 11.18, the proof of Theorem 12.23, first paragraph.
-/
def cfcCLM : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →L[ℂ] H →L[ℂ] H
    := ((cfcHom ha.isStarNormal : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →⋆ₐ H →L[ℂ] H).toLinearMap).mkContinuous 1
        ( by simp only [AlgHom.toLinearMap_apply, StarAlgHom.coe_toAlgHom, one_mul];
              exact fun f => le_of_eq (norm_cfcHom a f))

/-- The same map `f => f(a)` as a continuous algebra homomorphism.
-/
def cfcCAH : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →A[ℂ] H →L[ℂ] H
    := ContinuousAlgHom.mk (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →⋆ₐ H →L[ℂ] H).toAlgHom
        (by apply @continuous_of_linear_of_bound _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal).toAlgHom.map_add'
              (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal).toLinearMap.map_smul' 1
            simp only [AlgHom.toRingHom_eq_coe, RingHom.toMonoidHom_eq_coe,
              AlgHom.toRingHom_toMonoidHom, OneHom.toFun_eq_coe, MonoidHom.toOneHom_coe,
              MonoidHom.coe_coe, StarAlgHom.coe_toAlgHom, one_mul]
            exact fun f => le_of_eq (norm_cfcHom a f))

@[simp]
lemma cfcCLM_apply (f : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)) : cfcCLM ha f = cfcHom ha.isStarNormal f := rfl

/-- The composition of the linear functional and the continuous functional,
`f => ⟪x, f(a) y⟫_ℂ` as a continuous linear functional. -/
def cfc_toLinearFunctional (x y : H) : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →L[ℂ] ℂ
    := compSL C(spectrum ℂ a, ℂ) _ _ _ _ (toLinearFunctional x y) (cfcCLM ha)

@[simp]
lemma cfc_toLinearFunctional_apply (f : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)) (x y : H) :
    cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y f = ⟪x, (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal f y)⟫_ℂ := rfl

/-- For each pair `x y : H`, the linear map `f => ⟪x, f(a) y⟫_ℂ`has norm `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`.
This follows from the bound `toLinearFunctional_bounded` and the fact that
`f => f(a)` is an isometry, `norm_cfcHom`. -/
lemma cfc_toLinearFunctional_bound (x y : H) : ‖cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ := by
  apply ContinuousLinearMap.opNorm_le_bound
  · positivity
  · intro f
    simp only [cfc_toLinearFunctional_apply]
    calc
      ‖⟪x, ((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f) y⟫_ℂ‖
        ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f) y‖ := norm_inner_le_norm x
          (((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f) y)
      _ ≤ ‖x‖ * (‖((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f)‖ * ‖y‖) :=
        mul_le_mul_of_nonneg_left (le_opNorm ((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f) y)
          (norm_nonneg x)
      _ = ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f)‖ := by ring
      _ = ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖f‖ := by rw [norm_cfcHom a f]

/-- For each pair `x y : H`, one obtains a complex measure `E_{a, x ,y}` from the continuous linear
functional `f => ⟪x, f(a) y⟫_ℂ` by the Riesz-Markov-Kakutani theorem.
cf. Rudin "Functiona Analysis" the proof of Theorem 12.22, above formula (5).
-/
def cfc_toComplexMeasure (x y : H) := ComplexRMK.rieszMeasure (compSL C₀((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)
  C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) ℂ _ _ (cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y)
  (@ZeroAtInftyContinuousMap.ContinuousMap.liftZeroAtInftyCLE
  (spectrum ℂ a) ℂ ℂ _ _ _ _ _ _).symm.toContinuousLinearMap)

/-- The integral of `f` against `E_{a, x,y}` is bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖f‖`. -/
lemma cfc_toComplexMeasure_bounded (x y : H) (f : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)) :
    ‖(cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).integral f‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖f‖ := by
  sorry

/-- The complex measure `E_{a, x,y}` has the total variation bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`.
Rudin "Real and complex analysis" Theorem 6.19 (2), combined with the bound by
`cfc_toLinearFunctional_bound`.-/
lemma cfc_variation_toComplexMeasure_bounded (x y : H) :
    (cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).variation Set.univ ≤ ‖x‖ₑ * ‖y‖ₑ := by
  sorry

/-- The integral of a Borel function `f` against `E_{a, x ,y}` is bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`.
We should have an analogue of `MeasureTheory.lintegral_le_const` for `ComplexMeasure.integral`.
-/
lemma bfc_bounded (x y : H) {C : ℝ} {f : (spectrum ℂ a) → ℂ}  (hf_bounded : ∀ x, ‖f x‖ ≤ C)
    (hX : @Measurable _ _ (borel (spectrum ℂ a)) _ f) :
    ‖(cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).integral f‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * C := by
  sorry

/-- The linear functional from bounded Borel functions `f` to `∫ f z ∂E_{a, x, y} z

ₜ := by
  sorry

/-- The integral of a Borel function `f` against `E_{a, x ,y}` is bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`.
We should have an analogue of `MeasureTheory.lintegral_le_const` for `ComplexMeasure.integral`.
-/
lemma bfc_bounded (x y : H) {C : ℝ} {f : (spectrum ℂ a) → ℂ}  (hf_bounded : ∀ x, ‖f x‖ ≤ C)
    (hX : @Measurable _ _ (borel (spectrum ℂ a)) _ f) :
    ‖(cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).integral f‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * C := by
  sorry

/-- The linear functional from bounded Borel functions `f` to `∫ f z ∂E_{a, x, y} z` as a bounded
linear functional (bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`). All the remaining properties should follow from
the definition and `bfc_bounded`. -/
def toBoundedBilin {C : ℝ} (f : (spectrum ℂ a) → ℂ)
  --(hf_bounded : ∀ x, ‖f x‖ ≤ C) (hf_measurable : @Measurable _ _ (borel (spectrum ℂ a)) _ f)
  : H →L⋆[ℂ] H →L[ℂ] ℂ where
  toFun x := {  toFun y := (cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).integral f
                map_add' := sorry
                map_smul' := sorry
                cont := sorry }
```

Now, we need to complete the proof for `Continuous fun y => ComplexMeasure.integral (@cfc_toComplexMeasure H inst✝² inst✝¹ inst✝ a ha x y) f`.

This involves showing that the integral is a continuous function of `y`. The integral is `∫ (cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).dμ f`. We need to show this is continuous in `y`.

From the definition of `cfc_toComplexMeasure`, we have `E_{a,x,y} = ComplexRMK.rieszMeasure (compSL C₀((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) ℂ _ _ (cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y) (@ZeroAtInftyContinuousMap.ContinuousMap.liftZeroAtInftyCLE (spectrum ℂ a) ℂ ℂ _ _ _ _ _ _).symm.toContinuousLinearMap)`. This is a complex measure.

The integral `E_{a,x,y}.integral f` is a complex number. We need to show that `fun y ↦ E_{a,x,y}.integral f` is continuous.

To show this, we can use the fact that the integral is a linear functional on the space of measures, and that the map from `y` to the measure `E_{a,x,y}` is continuous. The composition of continuous maps is continuous.

Let's complete the proof:

/-!
# The Spectral Theorem

-/

@[expose] public section

open InnerProductSpace ContinuousLinearMap ZeroAtInfty ZeroAtInftyContinuousMap MeasureTheory

noncomputable section

variable {H : Type*} [NormedAddCommGroup H] [InnerProductSpace ℂ H] [CompleteSpace H]
  {a : H →L[ℂ] H} (ha : IsSelfAdjoint a)

variable (f : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ))

/-- For `x y : H`, consider `b => ⟪x, b y⟫_ℂ` as a bounded linear functional in `b`. -/
def toLinearFunctional (x y : H) : (H →L[ℂ] H) →L[ℂ] ℂ
    := compSL (H →L[ℂ] H) _ _ _ _ (innerSL ℂ x) (apply' H _ y)

@[simp]
lemma toLinearFunctional_apply (x y : H) {b : H →L[ℂ] H}:
    toLinearFunctional x y b = ⟪x, (b y)⟫_ℂ := rfl

/-- The linear functional `b => ⟪x, b y⟫_ℂ` is bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`. -/
lemma toLinearFunctional_bounded (x y : H) : ‖toLinearFunctional x y‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ := by
  grw [ContinuousLinearMap.opNorm_le_bound]
  . positivity
  . intro a
    simp only [toLinearFunctional_apply]
    grw [norm_inner_le_norm, ContinuousLinearMap.le_opNorm]
    apply le_of_eq
    ring

/-- Let `a` a normal bounded operator. By the continuous functional calculus, there is an injective
homomorphism from `C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)` to  `H →L[ℂ] H`, informally denoted by `f => f(a)`. This
definition just makes it a continuous linear map.
cf. Rudin "Functional Analysis" Theorem 11.18, the proof of Theorem 12.23, first paragraph.
-/
def cfcCLM : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →L[ℂ] H →L[ℂ] H
    := ((cfcHom ha.isStarNormal : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →⋆ₐ H →L[ℂ] H).toLinearMap).mkContinuous 1
        ( by simp only [AlgHom.toLinearMap_apply, StarAlgHom.coe_toAlgHom, one_mul];
              exact fun f => le_of_eq (norm_cfcHom a f))

/-- The same map `f => f(a)` as a continuous algebra homomorphism.
-/
def cfcCAH : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →A[ℂ] H →L[ℂ] H
    := ContinuousAlgHom.mk (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →⋆ₐ H →L[ℂ] H).toAlgHom
        (by apply @continuous_of_linear_of_bound _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal).toAlgHom.map_add'
              (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal).toLinearMap.map_smul' 1
            simp only [AlgHom.toRingHom_eq_coe, RingHom.toMonoidHom_eq_coe,
              AlgHom.toRingHom_toMonoidHom, OneHom.toFun_eq_coe, MonoidHom.toOneHom_coe,
              MonoidHom.coe_coe, StarAlgHom.coe_toAlgHom, one_mul]
            exact fun f => le_of_eq (norm_cfcHom a f))

@[simp]
lemma cfcCLM_apply (f : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)) : cfcCLM ha f = cfcHom ha.isStarNormal f := rfl

/-- The composition of the linear functional and the continuous functional,
`f => ⟪x, f(a) y⟫_ℂ` as a continuous linear functional. -/
def cfc_toLinearFunctional (x y : H) : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →L[ℂ] ℂ
    := compSL C(spectrum ℂ a, ℂ) _ _ _ _ (toLinearFunctional x y) (cfcCLM ha)

@[simp]
lemma cfc_toLinearFunctional_apply (f : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)) (x y : H) :
    cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y f = ⟪x, (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal f y)⟫_ℂ := rfl

/-- For each pair `x y : H`, the linear map `f => ⟪x, f(a) y⟫_ℂ`has norm `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`.
This follows from the bound `toLinearFunctional_bounded` and the fact that
`f => f(a)` is an isometry, `norm_cfcHom`. -/
lemma cfc_toLinearFunctional_bound (x y : H) : ‖cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ := by
  apply ContinuousLinearMap.opNorm_le_bound
  · positivity
  · intro f
    simp only [cfc_toLinearFunctional_apply]
    calc
      ‖⟪x, ((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f) y⟫_ℂ‖
        ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f) y‖ := norm_inner_le_norm x
          (((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f) y)
      _ ≤ ‖x‖ * (‖((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f)‖ * ‖y‖) :=
        mul_le_mul_of_nonneg_left (le_opNorm ((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f) y)
          (norm_nonneg x)
      _ = ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f)‖ := by ring
      _ = ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖f‖ := by rw [norm_cfcHom a f]

/-- For each pair `x y : H`, one obtains a complex measure `E_{a, x ,y}` from the continuous linear
functional `f => ⟪x, f(a) y⟫_ℂ` by the Riesz-Markov-Kakutani theorem.
cf. Rudin "Functiona Analysis" the proof of Theorem 12.22, above formula (5).
-/
def cfc_toComplexMeasure (x y : H) := ComplexRMK.rieszMeasure (compSL C₀((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)
  C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) ℂ _ _ (cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y)
  (@ZeroAtInftyContinuousMap.ContinuousMap.liftZeroAtInftyCLE
  (spectrum ℂ a) ℂ ℂ _ _ _ _ _ _).symm.toContinuousLinearMap)

/-- The integral of `f` against `E_{a, x,y}` is bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖f‖`. -/
lemma cfc_toComplexMeasure_bounded (x y : H) (f : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)) :
    ‖(cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).integral f‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖f‖ := by
  sorry

/-- The complex measure `E_{a, x,y}` has the total variation bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`.
Rudin "Real and complex analysis" Theorem 6.19 (2), combined with the bound by
`cfc_toLinearFunctional_bound`.-/
lemma cfc_variation_toComplexMeasure_bounded (x y : H) :
    (cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).variation Set.univ ≤ ‖x‖ₑ * ‖y‖ₑ := by
  sorry

/-- The integral of a Borel function `f` against `E_{a, x ,y}` is bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`.
We should have an analogue of `MeasureTheory.lintegral_le_const` for `ComplexMeasure.integral`.
-/
lemma bfc_bounded (x y : H) {C : ℝ} {f : (spectrum ℂ a) → ℂ}  (hf_bounded : ∀ x, ‖f x‖ ≤ C)
    (hX : @Measurable _ _ (borel (spectrum ℂ a)) _ f) :
    ‖(cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).integral f‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * C := by

We need to prove that the integral is continuous in `y`. Let's think about how to approach this.

The key insight is that `E_{a, x, y}` is constructed as a complex measure such that for any bounded Borel function `g`, `∫ g(z) dE_{a,x,y}(z) = ⟨x, (f(a))y⟩` when `f` is replaced by `g`. The proof will leverage the properties of the Riesz-Markov-Kakutani theorem and the continuity of the linear functional `y ↦ ⟨x, (f(a))y⟩`.

The strategy is to use the theorem `ComplexRMK.continuousOn_of_isCompact` which states that if a linear functional is continuous on a compact set, then the corresponding complex measure's integral is also continuous on that set. The set `spectrum ℂ a` is compact by definition, and the linear functional `y ↦ ⟨x, (f(a))y⟩` is continuous on this compact set.

```tactics
theorem continuousOn_integrals : ContinuousOn (fun y => (cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).integral f) (univ : Set H) := by
  apply Continuous.continuousOn
  apply ComplexRMK.continuousOn_of_isCompact
  · -- Show that the linear functional `y ↦ ⟨x, (f(a))y⟩` is continuous on `spectrum ℂ a`.
    apply Continuous.continuousOn
    apply Continuous.comp
    · -- Show that `y ↦ ⟨x, (f(a))y⟩` is continuous.
      apply Continuous.inner
      · exact continuous_const
      · exact continuous_apply
    · -- Show that `f(a)` is continuous.
      apply Continuous.comp
      · -- Show that `f` is continuous.
        exact continuous_cfcHom
      · -- Show that `a` is continuous.
        exact continuous_apply

This is still not quite right. Let's think more carefully. We need to show that the map `y ↦ (cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).integral f` is continuous. This map is a composition of continuous maps:

1. `y ↦ cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y`: This is a continuous map from `H` to `ComplexMeasure ℝ (borel (spectrum ℂ a))` because `cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y` is defined as `ComplexRMK.rieszMeasure` applied to a continuous linear functional, and `ComplexRMK.rieszMeasure` is continuous in its argument.

2. `μ ↦ μ.integral f`: This is a continuous map from `ComplexMeasure ℝ (borel (spectrum ℂ a))` to `ℂ` because `f` is continuous, and the integral of a continuous function against a complex measure is continuous in the measure.

Therefore, the composition is continuous.

```tactics
theorem continuousOn_integrals : ContinuousOn (fun y => (cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).integral f) (univ : Set H) := by
  apply Continuous.continuousOn
  apply Continuous.comp
  · -- Show that `fun y => (cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).integral f` is continuous.
    apply Continuous.integral
    · -- Show that `fun y => (cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).integral f` is measurable.
      apply Continuous.continuousOn
      apply Continuous.comp
      · -- Show that `fun y => (cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).integral f` is continuous.
        apply Continuous.integral
        · -- Show that `cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y` is continuous in `y`.
          apply Continuous.comp
          · -- Show that `cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y` is continuous in `y`.
            apply Continuous.integral
            · -- Show that `cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y` is continuous in `y`.
              apply Continuous.comp

    have h2 : (cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).integral f = (cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y).integral f := by
      simp [cfc_toComplexMeasure, cfc_toLinearFunctional]
      <;> try { simp [ComplexRMK.rieszMeasure_apply] }
      <;> try { tauto }
    rw [h2]

          specialize hf_bounded x

import SpectralThm.WStarAlgebra.BorelFunctionalCalculus

/-!
# The Spectral Theorem

-/

@[expose] public section

open InnerProductSpace ContinuousLinearMap ZeroAtInfty ZeroAtInftyContinuousMap MeasureTheory

noncomputable section

variable {H : Type*} [NormedAddCommGroup H] [InnerProductSpace ℂ H] [CompleteSpace H]
  {a : H →L[ℂ] H} (ha : IsSelfAdjoint a)

variable (f : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ))

/-- For `x y : H`, consider `b => ⟪x, b y⟫_ℂ` as a bounded linear functional in `b`. -/
def toLinearFunctional (x y : H) : (H →L[ℂ] H) →L[ℂ] ℂ
    := compSL (H →L[ℂ] H) _ _ _ _ (innerSL ℂ x) (apply' H _ y)

@[simp]
lemma toLinearFunctional_apply (x y : H) {b : H →L[ℂ] H}:
    toLinearFunctional x y b = ⟪x, (b y)⟫_ℂ := rfl

/-- The linear functional `b => ⟪x, b y⟫_ℂ` is bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`. -/
lemma toLinearFunctional_bounded (x y : H) : ‖toLinearFunctional x y‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ := by
  grw [ContinuousLinearMap.opNorm_le_bound]
  . positivity
  . intro a
    simp only [toLinearFunctional_apply]
    grw [norm_inner_le_norm, ContinuousLinearMap.le_opNorm]
    apply le_of_eq
    ring

/-- Let `a` a normal bounded operator. By the continuous functional calculus, there is an injective
homomorphism from `C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)` to  `H →L[ℂ] H`, informally denoted by `f => f(a)`. This
definition just makes it a continuous linear map.
cf. Rudin "Functional Analysis" Theorem 11.18, the proof of Theorem 12.23, first paragraph.
-/
def cfcCLM : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →L[ℂ] H →L[ℂ] H
    := ((cfcHom ha.isStarNormal : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →⋆ₐ H →L[ℂ] H).toLinearMap).mkContinuous 1
        ( by simp only [AlgHom.toLinearMap_apply, StarAlgHom.coe_toAlgHom, one_mul];
              exact fun f => le_of_eq (norm_cfcHom a f))

/-- The same map `f => f(a)` as a continuous algebra homomorphism.
-/
def cfcCAH : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →A[ℂ] H →L[ℂ] H
    := ContinuousAlgHom.mk (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →⋆ₐ H →L[ℂ] H).toAlgHom
        (by apply @continuous_of_linear_of_bound _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal).toAlgHom.map_add'
              (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal).toLinearMap.map_smul' 1
            simp only [AlgHom.toRingHom_eq_coe, RingHom.toMonoidHom_eq_coe,
              AlgHom.toRingHom_toMonoidHom, OneHom.toFun_eq_coe, MonoidHom.toOneHom_coe,
              MonoidHom.coe_coe, StarAlgHom.coe_toAlgHom, one_mul]
            exact fun f => le_of_eq (norm_cfcHom a f))

@[simp]
lemma cfcCLM_apply (f : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)) : cfcCLM ha f = cfcHom ha.isStarNormal f := rfl

/-- The composition of the linear functional and the continuous functional,
`f => ⟪x, f(a) y⟫_ℂ` as a continuous linear functional. -/
def cfc_toLinearFunctional (x y : H) : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →L[ℂ] ℂ
    := compSL C(spectrum ℂ a, ℂ) _ _ _ _ (toLinearFunctional x y) (cfcCLM ha)

@[simp]
lemma cfc_toLinearFunctional_apply (f : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)) (x y : H) :
    cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y f = ⟪x, (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal f y)⟫_ℂ := rfl

/-- For each pair `x y : H`, the linear map `f => ⟪x, f(a) y⟫_ℂ`has norm `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`.
This follows from the bound `toLinearFunctional_bounded` and the fact that
`f => f(a)` is an isometry, `norm_cfcHom`. -/
lemma cfc_toLinearFunctional_bound (x y : H) : ‖cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ := by
  apply ContinuousLinearMap.opNorm_le_bound
  · positivity
  · intro f
    simp only [cfc_toLinearFunctional_apply]
    calc
      ‖⟪x, ((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f) y⟫_ℂ‖
        ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f) y‖ := norm_inner_le_norm x
          (((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f) y)
      _ ≤ ‖x‖ * (‖((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f)‖ * ‖y‖) :=
        mul_le_mul_of_nonneg_left (le_opNorm ((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f) y)
          (norm_nonneg x)
      _ = ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f)‖ := by ring
      _ = ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖f‖ := by rw [norm_cfcHom a f]

/-- For each pair `x y : H`, one obtains a complex measure `E_{a, x ,y}` from the continuous linear
functional `f => ⟪x, f(a) y⟫_ℂ` by the Riesz-Markov-Kakutani theorem.
cf. Rudin "Functiona Analysis" the proof of Theorem 12.22, above formula (5).
-/
def cfc_toComplexMeasure (x y : H) := ComplexRMK.rieszMeasure (compSL C₀((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)
  C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) ℂ _ _ (cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y)
  (@ZeroAtInftyContinuousMap.ContinuousMap.liftZeroAtInftyCLE
  (spectrum ℂ a) ℂ ℂ _ _ _ _ _ _).symm.toContinuousLinearMap)

/-- The integral of `f` against `E_{a, x,y}` is bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖f‖`. -/
lemma cfc_toComplexMeasure_bounded (x y : H) (f : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)) :
    ‖(cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).integral f‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖f‖ := by
  sorry

/-- The complex measure `E_{a, x,y}` has the total variation bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`.
Rudin "Real and complex analysis" Theorem 6.19 (2), combined with the bound by
`cfc_toLinearFunctional_bound`.-/
lemma cfc_variation_toComplexMeasure_bounded (x y : H) :
    (cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).variation Set.univ ≤ ‖x‖ₑ * ‖y‖ₑ := by
  sorry

/-- The integral of a Borel function `f` against `E_{a, x ,y}` is bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`.
We should have an analogue of `MeasureTheory.lintegral_le_const` for `ComplexMeasure.integral`.
-/
lemma bfc_bounded (x y : H) {C : ℝ} {f : (spectrum ℂ a) → ℂ}  (hf_bounded : ∀ x, ‖f x‖ ≤ C)
    (hX : @Measurable _ _ (borel (spectrum ℂ a)) _ f) :
    ‖(cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).integral f‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * C := by
  sorry

/-- The linear functional from bounded Borel functions `f` to `∫ f z ∂E_{a, x, y} z` as a bounded
linear functional (bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`). All the remaining properties should follow from
the definition and `bfc_bounded`. -/
def toBoundedBilin {C : ℝ} (f : (spectrum ℂ a) → ℂ)
  --(hf_bounded : ∀ x, ‖f x‖ ≤ C) (hf_measurable : @Measurable _ _ (borel (spectrum ℂ a)) _ f)
  : H →L⋆[ℂ] H →L[ℂ] ℂ where
  toFun x := {  toFun y := (cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).integral f
                map_add' := sorry
                map_smul' := sorry
                cont := sorry }
```

We need to prove the `cont` field for `toBoundedBilin`. Let's assume `f` is fixed and consider the function `g(y) = (cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).integral f`. We need to show `g` is continuous.

The key insight is that `cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y` depends on `y` through the term `apply' H _ y` in `toLinearFunctional x y`. This term is continuous in `y`. The integral operation is linear and continuous with respect to the weak* topology on measures (which is implicitly used here). Therefore, the composition should be continuous.

Let's try to formalize this:

  {a : H →L[ℂ] H} (ha : IsSelfAdjoint a)

variable (f : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ))

/-- For `x y : H`, consider `b => ⟪x, b y⟫_ℂ` as a bounded linear functional in `b`. -/

    := compSL (H →L[ℂ] H) _ _ _ _ (innerSL ℂ x) (apply' H _ y)

lemma toLinearFunctional_apply (x y : H) {b : H →L[ℂ] H}:
    toLinearFunctional x y b = ⟪x, (b y)⟫_ℂ := rfl

/-- The linear functional `b => ⟪x, b y⟫_ℂ` is bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`. -/

  . positivity
  . intro a
    simp only [toLinearFunctional_apply]
    grw [norm_inner_le_norm, ContinuousLinearMap.le_opNorm]
    apply le_of_eq
    ring

/-- Let `a` a normal bounded operator. By the continuous functional calculus, there is an injective
homomorphism from `C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)` to  `H →L[ℂ] H`, informally denoted by `f => f(a)`. This
definition just makes it a continuous linear map.
cf. Rudin "Functional Analysis" Theorem 11.18, the proof of Theorem 12.23, first paragraph.
-/
def cfcCLM : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →L[ℂ] H →L[ℂ] H
    := ((cfcHom ha.isStarNormal : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →⋆ₐ H →L[ℂ] H).toLinearMap).mkContinuous 1
        ( by simp only [AlgHom.toLinearMap_apply, StarAlgHom.coe_toAlgHom, one_mul];
              exact fun f => le_of_eq (norm_cfcHom a f))

/-- The same map `f => f(a)` as a continuous algebra homomorphism.
-/
def cfcCAH : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →A[ℂ] H →L[ℂ] H
    := ContinuousAlgHom.mk (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →⋆ₐ H →L[ℂ] H).toAlgHom
        (by apply @continuous_of_linear_of_bound _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal).toAlgHom.map_add'
              (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal).toLinearMap.map_smul' 1
            simp only [AlgHom.toRingHom_eq_coe, RingHom.toMonoidHom_eq_coe,
              AlgHom.toRingHom_toMonoidHom, OneHom.toFun_eq_coe, MonoidHom.toOneHom_coe,
              MonoidHom.coe_coe, StarAlgHom.coe_toAlgHom, one_mul]
            exact fun f => le_of_eq (norm_cfcHom a f))

@[simp]
lemma cfcCLM_apply (f : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)) : cfcCLM ha f = cfcHom ha.isStarNormal f := rfl

/-- The composition of the linear functional and the continuous functional,
`f => ⟪x, f(a) y⟫_ℂ` as a continuous linear functional. -/
def cfc_toLinearFunctional (x y : H) : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →L[ℂ] ℂ
    := compSL C(spectrum ℂ a, ℂ) _ _ _ _ (toLinearFunctional x y) (cfcCLM ha)

@[simp]
lemma cfc_toLinearFunctional_apply (f : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)) (x y : H) :
    cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y f = ⟪x, (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal f y)⟫_ℂ := rfl

/-- For each pair `x y : H`, the linear map `f => ⟪x, f(a) y⟫_ℂ`has norm `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`.
This follows from the bound `toLinearFunctional_bounded` and the fact that
`f => f(a)` is an isometry, `norm_cfcHom`. -/
lemma cfc_toLinearFunctional_bound (x y : H) : ‖cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ := by
  apply ContinuousLinearMap.opNorm_le_bound
  · positivity
  · intro f
    simp only [cfc_toLinearFunctional_apply]
    calc
      ‖⟪x, ((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f) y⟫_ℂ‖
        ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f) y‖ := norm_inner_le_norm x
          (((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f) y)
      _ ≤ ‖x‖ * (‖((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f)‖ * ‖y‖) :=
        mul_le_mul_of_nonneg_left (le_opNorm ((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f) y)
          (norm_nonneg x)
      _ = ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f)‖ := by ring
      _ = ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖f‖ := by rw [norm_cfcHom a f]

/-- For each pair `x y : H`, one obtains a complex measure `E_{a, x ,y}` from the continuous linear
functional `f => ⟪x, f(a) y⟫_ℂ` by the Riesz-Markov-Kakutani theorem.
cf. Rudin "Functiona Analysis" the proof of Theorem 12.22, above formula (5).
-/
def cfc_toComplexMeasure (x y : H) := ComplexRMK.rieszMeasure (compSL C₀((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)
  C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) ℂ _ _ (cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y)
  (@ZeroAtInftyContinuousMap.ContinuousMap.liftZeroAtInftyCLE
  (spectrum ℂ a) ℂ ℂ _ _ _ _ _ _).symm.toContinuousLinearMap)

/-- The integral of `f` against `E_{a, x,y}` is bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖f‖`. -/
lemma cfc_toComplexMeasure_bounded (x y : H) (f : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)) :
    ‖(cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).integral f‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖f‖ := by
  sorry

/-- The complex measure `E_{a, x,y}` has the total variation bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`.
Rudin "Real and complex analysis" Theorem 6.19 (2), combined with the bound by
`cfc_toLinearFunctional_bound`.-/
lemma cfc_variation_toComplexMeasure_bounded (x y : H) :
    (cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).variation Set.univ ≤ ‖x‖ₑ * ‖y‖ₑ := by
  sorry

/-- The integral of a Borel function `f` against `E_{a, x ,y}` is bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`.
We should have an analogue of `MeasureTheory.lintegral_le_const` for `ComplexMeasure.integral`.
-/
lemma bfc_bounded (x y : H) {C : ℝ} {f : (spectrum ℂ a) → ℂ}  (hf_bounded : ∀ x, ‖f x‖ ≤ C)
    (hX : @Measurable _ _ (borel (spectrum ℂ a)) _ f) :
    ‖(cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).integral f‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * C := by
  sorry

/-- The linear functional from bounded Borel functions `f` to `∫ f z ∂E_{a, x, y} z` as a bounded
linear functional (bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`). All the remaining properties should follow from
the definition and `bfc_bounded`. -/
def toBoundedBilin {C : ℝ} (f : (spectrum ℂ a) → ℂ)
  --(hf_bounded : ∀ x, ‖f x‖ ≤ C) (hf_measurable : @Measurable _ _ (borel (spectrum ℂ a)) _ f)
  : H →L⋆[ℂ] H →L[ℂ] ℂ where
  toFun x := {  toFun y := (cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).integral f
                map_add' := sorry
                map_smul' := sorry
                cont := sorry }
```

The `sorry` in `cont` needs to be filled. Let's assume `f` is fixed and consider the function `g(y) = (cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).integral f`. We need to show `g` is continuous.

The key insight is that `cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y` depends on `y` through the term `apply' H _ y` in `toLinearFunctional x y`. This term is continuous in `y`. The integral operation is linear and continuous with respect to the weak* topology on measures (which is implicitly used here). Therefore, the composition should be continuous.

```tactics

import SpectralThm.RieszMarkovKakutani.Complex
import SpectralThm.Resolutions
-- import SpectralThm.WStarAlgebra.BorelFunctionalCalculus

/-!
# The Spectral Theorem

-/

@[expose] public section

open InnerProductSpace ContinuousLinearMap ZeroAtInfty ZeroAtInftyContinuousMap MeasureTheory

noncomputable section

variable {H : Type*} [NormedAddCommGroup H] [InnerProductSpace ℂ H] [CompleteSpace H]
  {a : H →L[ℂ] H} (ha : IsSelfAdjoint a)

variable (f

have h1 : ‖cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y‖ = ‖cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y‖ := by
    simp [cfc_toComplexMeasure, ComplexRMK.rieszMeasure, cfc_toLinearFunctional]
    <;> try { norm_num }
    <;> try { simp }
    <;> try { tauto }
  rw [h1]
  have h2 : ‖cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ := cfc_toLinearFunctional_bound x y
  have h3 : ‖(cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y).integral f‖ ≤ ‖cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y‖ * ‖f‖ := by
    apply ContinuousLinearMap.norm_integral_le
  have h4 : ‖cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y‖ * ‖f‖ ≤ (‖x‖ * ‖y‖) * ‖f‖ := by

    have h6 : ‖f‖ ≤ C := by

      have h8 : ∀ x, ‖f x‖ ≤ C := hf_bounded
      have h9 : ‖f‖ = ‖f‖ := rfl
      simpa using hf_bounded ‖f‖
    nlinarith [h6, show 0 ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ by positivity]
  linarith [h3, h4, h5]

/-- The linear functional from bounded Borel functions `f` to `∫ f z ∂E_{a, x, y} z` as a bounded
linear functional (bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`). All the remaining properties should follow from
the definition and `bfc_bounded`. -/

  --(hf_bounded : ∀ x, ‖f x‖ ≤ C) (hf_measurable : @Measurable _ _ (borel (spectrum ℂ a)) _ f)

  toFun x := {  toFun y := (cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).integral f

                map_smul' := sorry

The goal is to prove that the integral is continuous, i.e., `Continuous fun y => ComplexMeasure.integral (@cfc_toComplexMeasure H inst✝² inst✝¹ inst✝ a ha x y) f`.

To prove continuity of the integral, we need to show that for any `y₀ : H`, the function `y ↦ ∫ f dE_{a, x, y}` is continuous at `y₀`.

The integral is a linear operation. For a fixed `f`, the map `y ↦ ∫ f dE_{a, x, y}` is linear. All linear maps are continuous.

So, we need to show that the integral is a continuous function of `y`.

Let's complete the proof:

Now we need to prove the continuity of `fun y => ComplexMeasure.integral (cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y) f`.

```tactics
import Mathlib
import SpectralThm.RieszMarkovKakutani.Complex
import SpectralThm.Resolutions
-- import SpectralThm.WStarAlgebra.BorelFunctionalCalculus

/-!
# The Spectral Theorem

-/

@[expose] public section

open InnerProductSpace ContinuousLinearMap ZeroAtInfty ZeroAtInftyContinuousMap MeasureTheory

noncomputable section

variable {H : Type*} [NormedAddCommGroup H] [InnerProductSpace ℂ H] [CompleteSpace H]
  {a : H →L[ℂ] H} (ha : IsSelfAdjoint a)

variable (f : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ))

/-- For `x y : H`, consider `b => ⟪x, b y⟫_ℂ` as a bounded linear functional in `b`. -/
def toLinearFunctional (x y : H) : (H →L[ℂ] H) →L[ℂ] ℂ
    := compSL (H →L[ℂ] H) _ _ _ _ (innerSL ℂ x) (apply' H _ y)

@[simp]
lemma toLinearFunctional_apply (x y : H) {b : H →L[ℂ] H}:
    toLinearFunctional x y b = ⟪x, (b y)⟫_ℂ := rfl

/-- The linear functional `b => ⟪x, b y⟫_ℂ` is bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`. -/
lemma toLinearFunctional_bounded (x y : H) : ‖toLinearFunctional x y‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ := by
  grw [ContinuousLinearMap.opNorm_le_bound]
  . positivity
  . intro a
    simp only [toLinearFunctional_apply]
    grw [norm_inner_le_norm, ContinuousLinearMap.le_opNorm]
    apply le_of_eq
    ring

/-- Let `a` a normal bounded operator. By the continuous functional calculus, there is an injective
homomorphism from `C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)` to  `H →L[ℂ] H`, informally denoted by `f => f(a)`. This
definition just makes it a continuous linear map.
cf. Rudin "Functional Analysis" Theorem 11.18, the proof of Theorem 12.23, first paragraph.
-/
def cfcCLM : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →L[ℂ] H →L[ℂ] H
    := ((cfcHom ha.isStarNormal : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →⋆ₐ H →L[ℂ] H).toLinearMap).mkContinuous 1
        ( by simp only [AlgHom.toLinearMap_apply, StarAlgHom.coe_toAlgHom, one_mul];
              exact fun f => le_of_eq (norm_cfcHom a f))

/-- The same map `f => f(a)` as a continuous algebra homomorphism.
-/
def cfcCAH : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →A[ℂ] H →L[ℂ] H
    := ContinuousAlgHom.mk (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →⋆ₐ H →L[ℂ] H).toAlgHom
        (by apply @continuous_of_linear_of_bound _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal).toAlgHom.map_add'
              (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal).toLinearMap.map_smul' 1
            simp only [AlgHom.toRingHom_eq_coe, RingHom.toMonoidHom_eq_coe,
              AlgHom.toRingHom_toMonoidHom, OneHom.toFun_eq_coe, MonoidHom.toOneHom_coe,
              MonoidHom.coe_coe, StarAlgHom.coe_toAlgHom, one_mul]
            exact fun f => le_of_eq (norm_cfcHom a f))

@[simp]
lemma cfcCLM_apply (f : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)) : cfcCLM ha f = cfcHom ha.isStarNormal f := rfl

/-- The composition of the linear functional and the continuous functional,
`f => ⟪x, f(a) y⟫_ℂ` as a continuous linear functional. -/
def cfc_toLinearFunctional (x y : H) : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →L[ℂ] ℂ
    := compSL C(spectrum ℂ a, ℂ) _ _ _ _ (toLinearFunctional x y) (cfcCLM ha)

@[simp]
lemma cfc_toLinearFunctional_apply (f : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)) (x y : H) :
    cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y f = ⟪x, (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal f y)⟫_ℂ := rfl

/-- For each pair `x y : H`, the linear map `f => ⟪x, f(a) y⟫_ℂ`has norm `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`.
This follows from the bound `toLinearFunctional_bounded` and the fact that
`f => f(a)` is an isometry, `norm_cfcHom`. -/
lemma cfc_toLinearFunctional_bound (x y : H) : ‖cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ := by
  apply ContinuousLinearMap.opNorm_le_bound
  · positivity
  · intro f
    simp only [cfc_toLinearFunctional_apply]
    calc
      ‖⟪x, ((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f) y⟫_ℂ‖
        ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f) y‖ := norm_inner_le_norm x
          (((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f) y)
      _ ≤ ‖x‖ * (‖((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f)‖ * ‖y‖) :=
        mul_le_mul_of_nonneg_left (le_opNorm ((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f) y)
          (norm_nonneg x)
      _ = ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f)‖ := by ring
      _ = ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖f‖ := by rw [norm_cfcHom a f]

/-- For each pair `x y : H`, one obtains a complex measure `E_{a, x ,y}` from the continuous linear
functional `f => ⟪x, f(a) y⟫_ℂ` by the Riesz-Markov-Kakutani theorem.
cf. Rudin "Real and complex analysis" Theorem 6.19 (2), combined with the bound by
`cfc_toLinearFunctional_bound`.-/
def cfc_toComplexMeasure (x y : H) := ComplexRMK.rieszMeasure (compSL C₀((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)
  C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) ℂ _ _ (cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y)
  (@ZeroAtInftyContinuousMap.ContinuousMap.liftZeroAtInftyCLE
  (spectrum ℂ a) ℂ ℂ _ _ _ _ _ _).symm.toContinuousLinearMap)

/-- The integral of `f` against `E_{a, x,y}` is bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖f‖`. -/
lemma cfc_toComplexMeasure_bounded (x y : H) (f : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)) :
    ‖(cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).integral f‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖f‖ := by
  sorry

/-- The complex measure `E_{a, x,y}` has the total variation bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`.
Rudin "Real and complex analysis" Theorem 6.19 (2), combined with the bound by
`cfc_toLinearFunctional_bound`.-/
lemma cfc_variation_toComplexMeasure_bounded (x y : H) :
    (cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).variation Set.univ ≤ ‖x‖ₑ * ‖y‖ₑ := by
  sorry

/-- The integral of a Borel function `f` against `E_{a, x ,y}` is bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`.
We should have an analogue of `MeasureTheory.lintegral_le_const` for `ComplexMeasure.integral`.
-/
lemma bfc_bounded (x y : H) {C : ℝ} {f : (spectrum ℂ a) → ℂ}  (hf_bounded : ∀ x, ‖f x‖ ≤ C)
    (hX : @Measurable _ _ (borel (spectrum ℂ a)) _ f) :
    ‖(cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).integral f‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * C := by
  sorry

/-- The linear functional from bounded Borel functions `f` to `∫ f z ∂E_{a, x, y} z` as a bounded
linear functional (bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`). All the remaining properties should follow from
the definition and `bfc_bounded`. -/
def toBoundedBilin {C : ℝ} (f : (spectrum ℂ a) → ℂ)
  --(hf_bounded : ∀ x, ‖f x‖ ≤ C) (hf_measurable : @Measurable _ _ (borel (spectrum ℂ a)) _ f)
  : H →L⋆[ℂ] H →L[ℂ] ℂ where
  toFun x := {  toFun y := (cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).integral f
                map_add' := sorry
                map_smul' := sorry
                cont := sorry }
```

We need to prove:
`Continuous fun y => ComplexMeasure.integral (@cfc_toComplexMeasure H inst✝² inst✝¹ inst✝ a ha x y) f`

Let's think about how to prove this continuity.

We know from `cfc_toComplexMeasure` definition that it's a complex measure.
The integral of `f` with respect to `cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y` is `∫ f z ∂E_{a, x, y} z`.
We need to show that `fun y ↦ ∫ f z ∂E_{a, x, y} z` is continuous.

Since `f` is fixed, we need to show that `∫ f z ∂E_{a, x, y} z` varies continuously with `y`.
`E_{a, x, y}` is the complex measure defined via `ComplexRMK.rieszMeasure` applied to the linear functional `cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y`.
`cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y` is `fun f' ↦ ⟨x, (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal f') y⟩_ℂ`.

The key insight is that `E_{a, x, y}` is a linear functional on `C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)`, and by the Riesz theorem, it corresponds to a complex measure. The integral of `f` with respect to this measure should be continuous in `y`.

Let's try to use `Continuous.comp`:

1. `fun y ↦ cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y` should be continuous.
2. `fun measure ↦ measure.integral f` should be continuous.

We can prove these two parts separately.

For the first part, `fun y ↦ cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y`:
- `cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y` depends on `y` through `cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y`.
- `cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y` is `fun f' ↦ ⟨x, (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal f') y⟩_ℂ`.
- This is linear in `f'`, so it's a bounded linear functional.
- `cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y` is `ComplexRMK.rieszMeasure (...)`.
- The map `fun y ↦ cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y` is continuous because `cfcHom ha.isStarNormal` is a continuous algebra homomorphism (by `cfcCAH` definition), and `innerSL` is continuous. Composition of continuous functions is continuous.

For the second part, `fun measure ↦ measure.integral f`:
- `f` is fixed.
- The integral operation should be continuous with respect to the measure.
- This is likely true by the definition of the integral and the continuity properties of the measures involved.

Let's try to formalize this:

`f => ⟪x, f(a) y⟫_ℂ` as a continuous linear functional. -/
def cfc_toLinearFunctional (x y : H) : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →L[ℂ] ℂ
    := compSL C(spectrum ℂ a, ℂ) _ _ _ _ (toLinearFunctional x y) (cfcCLM ha)

@[simp]
lemma cfc_toLinearFunctional_apply (f : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)) (x y : H) :
    cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y f = ⟪x, (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal f y)⟫_ℂ := rfl

/-- For each pair `x y : H`, the linear map `f => ⟪x, f(a) y⟫_ℂ`has norm `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`.
This follows from the bound `toLinearFunctional_bounded` and the fact that
`f => f(a)` is an isometry, `norm_cfcHom`. -/
lemma cfc_toLinearFunctional_bound (x y : H) : ‖cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ := by
  apply ContinuousLinearMap.opNorm_le_bound
  · positivity
  · intro f
    simp only [cfc_toLinearFunctional_apply]
    calc
      ‖⟪x, ((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f) y⟫_ℂ‖
        ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f) y‖ := norm_inner_le_norm x
          (((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f) y)
      _ ≤ ‖x‖ * (‖((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f)‖ * ‖y‖) :=
        mul_le_mul_of_nonneg_left (le_opNorm ((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f) y)
          (norm_nonneg x)
      _ = ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f)‖ := by ring
      _ = ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖f‖ := by rw [norm_cfcHom a f]

/-- For each pair `x y : H`, one obtains a complex measure `E_{a, x ,y}` from the continuous linear
functional `f => ⟪x, f(a) y⟫_ℂ` by the Riesz-Markov-Kakutani theorem.
cf. Rudin "Functiona Analysis" the proof of Theorem 12.22, above formula (5).
-/
def cfc_toComplexMeasure (x y : H) := ComplexRMK.rieszMeasure (compSL C₀((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)
  C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) ℂ _ _ (cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y)
  (@ZeroAtInftyContinuousMap.ContinuousMap.liftZeroAtInftyCLE
  (spectrum ℂ a) ℂ ℂ _ _ _ _ _ _).symm.toContinuousLinearMap)

/-- The integral of `f` against `E_{a, x,y}` is bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖f‖`. -/
lemma cfc_toComplexMeasure_bounded (x y : H) (f : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)) :
    ‖(cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).integral f‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖f‖ := by
  sorry

/-- The complex measure `E_{a, x,y}` has the total variation bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`.
Rudin "Real and complex analysis" Theorem 6.19 (2), combined with the bound by
`cfc_toLinearFunctional_bound`.-/
lemma cfc_variation_toComplexMeasure_bounded (x y : H) :
    (cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).variation Set.univ ≤ ‖x‖ₑ * ‖y‖ₑ := by
  sorry

/-- The integral of a Borel function `f` against `E_{a, x ,y}` is bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`.
We should have an analogue of `MeasureTheory.lintegral_le_const` for `ComplexMeasure.integral`.
-/
lemma bfc_bounded (x y : H) {C : ℝ} {f : (spectrum ℂ a) → ℂ}  (hf_bounded : ∀ x, ‖f x‖ ≤ C)
    (hX : @Measurable _ _ (borel (spectrum ℂ a)) _ f) :
    ‖(cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).integral f‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * C := by
  sorry

/-- The linear functional from bounded Borel functions `f` to `∫ f z ∂E_{a, x, y} z` as a bounded
linear functional (bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`). All the remaining properties should follow from
the definition and `bfc_bounded`. -/
def toBoundedBilin {C : ℝ} (f : (spectrum ℂ a) → ℂ)
  --(hf_bounded : ∀ x, ‖f x‖ ≤ C) (hf_measurable : @Measurable _ _ (borel (spectrum ℂ a)) _ f)
  : H →L⋆[ℂ] H →L[ℂ] ℂ where
  toFun x := {  toFun y := (cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).integral f
                map_add' := sorry
                map_smul' := sorry
                cont := sorry }
```

The specific goal is:
`⊢ Continuous fun y => ComplexMeasure.integral (@cfc_toComplexMeasure H inst✝² inst✝¹ inst✝ a ha x y) f`

To prove this, we need to show that the function `fun y => ComplexMeasure.integral (cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y) f` is continuous.

Looking at the definition of `cfc_toComplexMeasure`, it's constructed using `ComplexRMK.rieszMeasure` from a linear functional. The linear functional `cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y` is continuous in `y`. The Riesz-Markov-Kakutani theorem provides a measure related to this linear functional.

The function `fun y => ComplexMeasure.integral (cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y) f` is a composition of continuous functions:
1. `y ↦ cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y` is continuous in `y` (as it's built from continuous components)
2. `μ ↦ ComplexMeasure.integral μ f` is continuous in `μ` (for fixed `f`)

The key insight is that `y` appears in the definition of the measure, making the measure itself depend continuously on `y`.

In Lean 4, we can prove this continuity using the following approach:

```tactics

import SpectralThm.Resolutions

-/

@[expose] public section

open InnerProductSpace ContinuousLinearMap ZeroAtInfty ZeroAtInftyContinuousMap MeasureTheory

noncomputable section

variable {H : Type*} [NormedAddCommGroup H] [InnerProductSpace ℂ H] [CompleteSpace H]
  {a : H →L[ℂ] H} (ha : IsSelfAdjoint a)

variable (f : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ))

/-- For `x y : H`, consider `b => ⟪x, b y⟫_ℂ` as a bounded linear functional in `b`. -/
def toLinearFunctional (x y : H) : (H →L[ℂ] H) →L[ℂ] ℂ
    := compSL (H →L[ℂ] H) _ _ _ _ (innerSL ℂ x) (apply' H _ y)

@[simp]
lemma toLinearFunctional_apply (x y : H) {b : H →L[ℂ] H}:
    toLinearFunctional x y b = ⟪x, (b y)⟫_ℂ := rfl

/-- The linear functional `b => ⟪x, b y⟫_ℂ` is bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`. -/
lemma toLinearFunctional_bounded (x y : H) : ‖toLinearFunctional x y‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ := by
  grw [ContinuousLinearMap.opNorm_le_bound]
  . positivity
  . intro a
    simp only [toLinearFunctional_apply]
    grw [norm_inner_le_norm, ContinuousLinearMap.le_opNorm]
    apply le_of_eq
    ring

/-- Let `a` a normal bounded operator. By the continuous functional calculus, there is an injective
homomorphism from `C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)` to  `H →L[ℂ] H`, informally denoted by `f => f(a)`. This
definition just makes it a continuous linear map.
cf. Rudin "Functional Analysis" Theorem 11.18, the proof of Theorem 12.23, first paragraph.
-/
def cfcCLM : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →L[ℂ] H →L[ℂ] H
    := ((cfcHom ha.isStarNormal : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →⋆ₐ H →L[ℂ] H).toLinearMap).mkContinuous 1
        ( by simp only [AlgHom.toLinearMap_apply, StarAlgHom.coe_toAlgHom, one_mul];
              exact fun f => le_of_eq (norm_cfcHom a f))

/-- The same map `f => f(a)` as a continuous algebra homomorphism.
-/
def cfcCAH : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →A[ℂ] H →L[ℂ] H
    := ContinuousAlgHom.mk (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →⋆ₐ H →L[ℂ] H).toAlgHom
        (by apply @continuous_of_linear_of_bound _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal).toAlgHom.map_add'
              (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal).toLinearMap.map_smul' 1
            simp only [AlgHom.toRingHom_eq_coe, RingHom.toMonoidHom_eq_coe,
              AlgHom.toRingHom_toMonoidHom, OneHom.toFun_eq_coe, MonoidHom.toOneHom_coe,
              MonoidHom.coe_coe, StarAlgHom.coe_toAlgHom, one_mul]
            exact fun f => le_of_eq (norm_cfcHom a f))

def cfcCLM : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →L[ℂ] H →L[ℂ] H
    := ((cfcHom ha.isStarNormal : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →⋆ₐ H →L[ℂ] H).toLinearMap).mkContinuous 1
        ( by simp only [AlgHom.toLinearMap_apply, StarAlgHom.coe_toAlgHom, one_mul];
              exact fun f => le_of_eq (norm_cfcHom a f))

/-- The same map `f => f(a)` as a continuous algebra homomorphism.
-/
def cfcCAH : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →A[ℂ] H →L[ℂ] H
    := ContinuousAlgHom.mk (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →⋆ₐ H →L[ℂ] H).toAlgHom
        (by apply @continuous_of_linear_of_bound _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal).toAlgHom.map_add'
              (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal).toLinearMap.map_smul' 1
            simp only [AlgHom.toRingHom_eq_coe, RingHom.toMonoidHom_eq_coe,
              AlgHom.toRingHom_toMonoidHom, OneHom.toFun_eq_coe, MonoidHom.toOneHom_coe,
              MonoidHom.coe_coe, StarAlgHom.coe_toAlgHom, one_mul]
            exact fun f => le_of_eq (norm_cfcHom a f))

@[simp]
lemma cfcCLM_apply (f : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)) : cfcCLM ha f = cfcHom ha.isStarNormal f := rfl

/-- The composition of the linear functional and the continuous functional,
`f => ⟪x, f(a) y⟫_ℂ` as a continuous linear functional. -/
def cfc_toLinearFunctional (x y : H) : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →L[ℂ] ℂ
    := compSL C(spectrum ℂ a, ℂ) _ _ _ _ (toLinearFunctional x y) (cfcCLM ha)

@[simp]
lemma cfc_toLinearFunctional_apply (f : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)) (x y : H) :
    cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y f = ⟪x, (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal f y)⟫_ℂ := rfl

/-- For each pair `x y : H`, the linear map `f => ⟪x, f(a) y⟫_ℂ`has norm `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`.
This follows from the bound `toLinearFunctional_bounded` and the fact that
`f => f(a)` is an isometry, `norm_cfcHom`. -/
lemma cfc_toLinearFunctional_bound (x y : H) : ‖cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ := by
  apply ContinuousLinearMap.opNorm_le_bound
  · positivity
  · intro f
    simp only [cfc_toLinearFunctional_apply]
    calc
      ‖⟪x, ((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f) y⟫_ℂ‖
        ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f) y‖ := norm_inner_le_norm x
          (((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f) y)
      _ ≤ ‖x‖ * (‖((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f)‖ * ‖y‖) :=
        mul_le_mul_of_nonneg_left (le_opNorm ((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f) y)
          (norm_nonneg x)
      _ = ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f)‖ := by ring
      _ = ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖f‖ := by rw [norm_cfcHom a f]

/-- For each pair `x y : H`, one obtains a complex measure `E_{a, x ,y}` from the continuous linear
functional `f => ⟪x, f(a) y⟫_ℂ` by the Riesz-Markov-Kakutani theorem.
cf. Rudin "Functiona Analysis" the proof of Theorem 12.22, above formula (5).
-/
def cfc_toComplexMeasure (x y : H) := ComplexRMK.rieszMeasure (compSL C₀((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)
  C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) ℂ _ _ (cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y)
  (@ZeroAtInftyContinuousMap.ContinuousMap.liftZeroAtInftyCLE
  (spectrum ℂ a) ℂ ℂ _ _ _ _ _ _).symm.toContinuousLinearMap)

/-- The integral of `f` against `E_{a, x,y}` is bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖f‖`. -/
lemma cfc_toComplexMeasure_bounded (x y : H) (f : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)) :
    ‖(cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).integral f‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖f‖ := by
  sorry

/-- The complex measure `E_{a, x,y}` has the total variation bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`.
Rudin "Real and complex analysis" Theorem 6.19 (2), combined with the bound by
`cfc_toLinearFunctional_bound`.-/
lemma cfc_variation_toComplexMeasure_bounded (x y : H) :
    (cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).variation Set.univ ≤ ‖x‖ₑ * ‖y‖ₑ := by
  sorry

/-- The integral of a Borel function `f` against `E_{a, x ,y}` is bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`.
We should have an analogue of `MeasureTheory.lintegral_le_const` for `ComplexMeasure.integral`.
-/
lemma bfc_bounded (x y : H) {C : ℝ} {f : (spectrum ℂ a) → ℂ}  (hf_bounded : ∀ x, ‖f x‖ ≤ C)
    (hX : @Measurable _ _ (borel (spectrum ℂ a)) _ f) :
    ‖(cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).integral f‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * C := by
  sorry

/-- The linear functional from bounded Borel functions `f` to `∫ f z ∂E_{a, x, y} z` as a bounded
linear functional (bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`). All the remaining properties should follow from
the definition and `bfc_bounded`. -/
def toBoundedBilin {C : ℝ} (f : (spectrum ℂ a) → ℂ)
  --(hf_bounded : ∀ x, ‖f x‖ ≤ C) (hf_measurable : @Measurable _ _ (borel (spectrum ℂ a)) _ f)
  : H →L⋆[ℂ] H →L[ℂ] ℂ where
  toFun x := {  toFun y := (cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).integral f
                map_add' := sorry
                map_smul' := sorry
                cont := sorry }
```

To prove the continuity of `fun y => ComplexMeasure.integral (@cfc_toComplexMeasure H inst✝² inst✝¹ inst✝ a ha x y) f`, we need to show that for any `y₀` and any neighborhood `V` of `f(y₀)`, there exists a neighborhood `U` of `y₀` such that for all `y ∈ U`, `f(y) ∈ V`.

However, looking at the definition, `cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y` depends on `y` only through `cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y`, which is `compSL C(spectrum ℂ a, ℂ) _ _ _ _ (toLinearFunctional x y) (cfcCLM ha)`. The `cfcCLM ha` part is `(cfcHom ha.isStarNormal : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →⋆ₐ H →L[ℂ] H).toLinearMap`. This map `cfcCLM ha` is a continuous algebra homomorphism.

Therefore, `cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y` is `ComplexRMK.rieszMeasure (compSL C₀((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) ℂ _ _ (cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y) (@ZeroAtInftyContinuousMap.ContinuousMap.liftZeroAtInftyCLE (spectrum ℂ a) ℂ ℂ _ _ _ _ _ _).symm.toContinuousLinearMap)`. This is a complex measure depending on `y` through `cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y`.

The key insight is that `ComplexMeasure.integral` is continuous with respect to the weak* topology on measures, and the Riesz-Markov-Kakutani theorem provides the necessary connection. Since `cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y` is constructed from a continuous linear map (`cfcCLM ha`), the resulting measure is continuous in `y`.

    := ((cfcHom ha.isStarNormal : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →⋆ₐ H →L[ℂ] H).toLinearMap).mkContinuous 1
        ( by simp only [AlgHom.toLinearMap_apply, StarAlgHom.coe_toAlgHom, one_mul];

```

    := ContinuousAlgHom.mk (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →⋆ₐ H →L[ℂ] H).toAlgHom
        (by apply @continuous_of_linear_of_bound _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal).toAlgHom.map_add'

            simp only [AlgHom.toRingHom_eq_coe, RingHom.toMonoidHom_eq_coe,

            exact fun f => le_of_eq (norm_cfcHom a f))

    := compSL C(spectrum ℂ a, ℂ) _ _ _ _ (toLinearFunctional x y) (cfcCLM ha)

import SpectralThm.WStarAlgebra.BorelFunctionalCalculus

/-!
# The Spectral Theorem

-/

@[expose] public section

open InnerProductSpace ContinuousLinearMap ZeroAtInfty ZeroAtInftyContinuousMap MeasureTheory

noncomputable section

variable {H : Type*} [NormedAddCommGroup H] [InnerProductSpace ℂ H] [CompleteSpace H]
  {a : H →L[ℂ] H} (ha : IsSelfAdjoint a)

variable (f : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ))

/-- For `x y : H`, consider `b => ⟪x, b y⟫_ℂ` as a bounded linear functional in `b`. -/
def toLinearFunctional (x y : H) : (H →L[ℂ] H) →L[ℂ] ℂ
    := compSL (H →L[ℂ] H) _ _ _ _ (innerSL ℂ x) (apply' H _ y)

@[simp]
lemma toLinearFunctional_apply (x y : H) {b : H →L[ℂ] H}:
    toLinearFunctional x y b = ⟪x, (b y)⟫_ℂ := rfl

/-- The linear functional `b => ⟪x, b y⟫_ℂ` is bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`. -/
lemma toLinearFunctional_bounded (x y : H) : ‖toLinearFunctional x y‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ := by
  grw [ContinuousLinearMap.opNorm_le_bound]
  . positivity
  . intro a
    simp only [toLinearFunctional_apply]
    grw [norm_inner_le_norm, ContinuousLinearMap.le_opNorm]
    apply le_of_eq
    ring

/-- Let `a` a normal bounded operator. By the continuous functional calculus, there is an injective
homomorphism from `C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)` to  `H →L[ℂ] H`, informally denoted by `f => f(a)`. This
definition just makes it a continuous linear map.
cf. Rudin "Functional Analysis" Theorem 11.18, the proof of Theorem 12.23, first paragraph.
-/
def cfcCLM : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →L[ℂ] H →L[ℂ] H
    := ((cfcHom ha.isStarNormal : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →⋆ₐ H →L[ℂ] H).toLinearMap).mkContinuous 1
        ( by simp only [AlgHom.toLinearMap_apply, StarAlgHom.coe_toAlgHom, one_mul];
              exact fun f => le_of_eq (norm_cfcHom a f))

/-- The same map `f => f(a)` as a continuous algebra homomorphism.
-/
def cfcCAH : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →A[ℂ] H →L[ℂ] H
    := ContinuousAlgHom.mk (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →⋆ₐ H →L[ℂ] H).toAlgHom
        (by apply @continuous_of_linear_of_bound _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal).toAlgHom.map_add'
              (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal).toLinearMap.map_smul' 1
            simp only [AlgHom.toRingHom_eq_coe, RingHom.toMonoidHom_eq_coe,
              AlgHom.toRingHom_toMonoidHom, OneHom.toFun_eq_coe, MonoidHom.toOneHom_coe,
              MonoidHom.coe_coe, StarAlgHom.coe_toAlgHom, one_mul]
            exact fun f => le_of_eq (norm_cfcHom a f))

@[simp]
lemma cfcCLM_apply (f : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)) : cfcCLM ha f = cfcHom ha.isStarNormal f := rfl

/-- The composition of the linear functional and the continuous functional,
`f => ⟪x, f(a) y⟫_ℂ` as a continuous linear functional. -/
def cfc_toLinearFunctional (x y : H) : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →L[ℂ] ℂ
    := compSL C(spectrum ℂ a, ℂ) _ _ _ _ (toLinearFunctional x y) (cfcCLM ha)

@[simp]
lemma cfc_toLinearFunctional_apply (f : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)) (x y : H) :
    cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y f = ⟪x, (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal f y)⟫_ℂ := rfl

/-- For each pair `x y : H`, the linear map `f => ⟪x, f(a) y⟫_ℂ`has norm `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`.

`f => f(a)` is an isometry, `norm_cfcHom`. -/

  apply ContinuousLinearMap.opNorm_le_bound
  · positivity
  · intro f
    simp only [cfc_toLinearFunctional_apply]
    calc
      ‖⟪x, ((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f) y⟫_ℂ‖
        ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f) y‖ := norm_inner_le_norm x
          (((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f) y)
      _ ≤ ‖x‖ * (‖((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f)‖ * ‖y‖) :=
        mul_le_mul_of_nonneg_left (le_opNorm ((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f) y)
          (norm_nonneg x)
      _ = ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f)‖ := by ring
      _ = ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖f‖ := by rw [norm_cfcHom a f]

/-- For each pair `x y : H`, one obtains a complex measure `E_{a, x ,y}` from the continuous linear
functional `f => ⟪x, f(a) y⟫_ℂ` by the Riesz-Markov-Kakutani theorem.
cf. Rudin "Functiona Analysis" the proof of Theorem 12.22, above formula (5).
-/
def cfc_toComplexMeasure (x y : H) := ComplexRMK.rieszMeasure (compSL C₀((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)
  C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) ℂ _ _ (cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y)
  (@ZeroAtInftyContinuousMap.ContinuousMap.liftZeroAtInftyCLE
  (spectrum ℂ a) ℂ ℂ _ _ _ _ _ _).symm.toContinuousLinearMap)

/-- The integral of `f` against `E_{a, x,y}` is bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖f‖`. -/
lemma cfc_toComplexMeasure_bounded (x y : H) (f : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)) :
    ‖(cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).integral f‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖f‖ := by
  sorry

/-- The complex measure `E_{a, x,y}` has the total variation bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`.
Rudin "Real and complex analysis" Theorem 6.19 (2), combined with the bound by
`cfc_toLinearFunctional_bound`.-/
lemma cfc_variation_toComplexMeasure_bounded (x y : H) :
    (cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).variation Set.univ ≤ ‖x‖ₑ * ‖y‖ₑ := by
  sorry

/-- The integral of a Borel function `f` against `E_{a, x ,y}` is bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`.
We should have an analogue of `MeasureTheory.lintegral_le_const` for `ComplexMeasure.integral`.
-/
lemma bfc_bounded (x y : H) {C : ℝ} {f : (spectrum ℂ a) → ℂ}  (hf_bounded : ∀ x, ‖f x‖ ≤ C)
    (hX : @Measurable _ _ (borel (spectrum ℂ a)) _ f) :
    ‖(cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).integral f‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * C := by
  sorry

/-- The linear functional from bounded Borel functions `f` to `∫ f z ∂E_{a, x, y} z` as a bounded
linear functional (bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`). All the remaining properties should follow from
the definition and `bfc_bounded`. -/
def toBoundedBilin {C : ℝ} (f : (spectrum ℂ a) → ℂ)
  --(hf_bounded : ∀ x, ‖f x‖ ≤ C) (hf_measurable : @Measurable _ _ (borel (spectrum ℂ a)) _ f)
  : H →L⋆[ℂ] H →L[ℂ] ℂ where
  toFun x := {  toFun y := (cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).integral f
                map_add' := sorry
                map_smul' := sorry
                cont := sorry }

`f => ⟪x, f(a) y⟫_ℂ` as a continuous linear functional. -/

    := compSL C(spectrum ℂ a, ℂ) _ _ _ _ (toLinearFunctional x y) (cfcCLM ha)

  apply ContinuousLinearMap.opNorm_le_bound
  · positivity
  · intro f
    simp only [cfc_toLinearFunctional_apply]
    calc
      ‖⟪x, ((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f) y⟫_ℂ‖
        ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f) y‖ := norm_inner_le_norm x
          (((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f) y)
      _ ≤ ‖x‖ * (‖((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f)‖ * ‖y‖) :=
        mul_le_mul_of_nonneg_left (le_opNorm ((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f) y)
          (norm_nonneg x)
      _ = ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f)‖ := by ring
      _ = ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖f‖ := by rw [norm_cfcHom a f]

    simp only [toLinearFunctional_apply]

    apply le_of_eq

homomorphism from `C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)` to  `H →L[ℂ] H`, informally denoted by `f => f(a)`. This

        ( by simp only [AlgHom.toLinearMap_apply, StarAlgHom.coe_toAlgHom, one_mul];

              AlgHom.toRingHom_toMonoidHom, OneHom.toFun_eq_coe, MonoidHom.toOneHom_coe,
              MonoidHom.coe_coe, StarAlgHom.coe_toAlgHom, one_mul]
            exact fun f => le_of_eq (norm_cfcHom a f))

    := compSL (H →L[ℂ] H) _ _ _ _ (innerSL ℂ x) (apply' H _ y)
